name: Philodendron tortum 'Aurea Variegated'
origin: Northern Brazil to Bolivia; variegated collector form in cultivation.
aboutText: Philodendron tortum 'Aurea Variegated' is an exceptional climbing aroid native from northern Brazil to Bolivia. Prized for its highly unusual, deeply divided skeleton-like or lace-like leaves with many narrow, linear segments, this cultivated select form displays vibrant aurea (yellow to chartreuse) variegation in both sectoral patches and delicate marbling. It has a climbing growth habit, supported by smooth green petioles with sheathing nodes and sturdy aerial roots, and thrives in wet tropical setups with bright indirect light and high humidity.

quickFacts.light: Bright, indirect filter - higher light to maintain aurea colour

morphology.leafShape: Deeply pinnatisect, skeleton-like with narrow linear segments
morphology.venation: Central rachis with thin segment veins
morphology.variegation: Aurea (yellow to chartreuse) sectors and marbling
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with aerial roots at nodes

propagation.notes: Single-node cuttings root readily in moist sphagnum or water. Maintain 70%+ humidity and bottom warmth for best results.
